title: What to know about Melissa officinalis before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Melissa officinalis, commonly known as sweet melissa, is a herb used for its medicinal properties in treating health conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. It has main health benefits including stress relief, improved mood, and enhanced cognitive function. The herb's therapeutic properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Bioactive compounds present in Melissa officinalis include limonene, linalool, and rosmarinic acid. People commonly prepare the herb as a tea by infusing its leaves in hot water.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the health benefits of Zingiber officinale include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate fever, as evidenced by its reduction of carrageenan-induced paw swelling and yeast-induced fever in rats. Additionally, the extract has been found to inhibit the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria, demonstrating its antimicrobial properties. Furthermore, the study showed that Zingiber officinale has potential in managing blood glucose levels, as it produced blood glucose lowering in rabbits.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Melissa officinalis are relief from anxiety, which is attributed to the anxiolytic effects of rosmarinic acid, a phenolic dimer that has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety. The plant has also been used to alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea, due to its high content of apigenin and luteolin, flavonoids that have been demonstrated to have anti-inflammatory properties and improve gut health. Additionally, Melissa officinalis is used to treat respiratory issues, including fever, which is caused by the presence of essential oils like borneol and camphor, that exhibit antimicrobial and antipyretic properties. Furthermore, the plant's sedative properties, attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid and apigenin, make it effective in treating sleep disorders and insomnia. The plant's efficacy in alleviating menopausal symptoms and hiccups can also be attributed to its flavonoid content, which has been shown to have estrogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, respectively.

uses_list.description: This herb has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety due to its ability to influence the levels of stress hormones such as cortisol in the body, thereby promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
The herb contains compounds with carminative properties, which help to alleviate digestive issues such as bloating and gas by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive system.
It is often used to help individuals cope with stress due to its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to adapt to stressful situations and promote overall well-being.
Its calming effects and ability to promote relaxation make it a popular natural remedy for insomnia, helping individuals to fall asleep and maintain a restful sleep.
The herb's sedative properties help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, making it a useful natural remedy for various sleep disorders, including insomnia and restlessness.
A popular remedy for hiccups, the herb is often used to help alleviate this condition due to its soothing effects on the nervous system.
Its ability to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation makes it a useful natural remedy for diarrhea, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.
The herb has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings, due to its ability to influence hormone levels and promote overall well-being.
The herb has been used to help reduce fever due to its ability to promote sweating, which helps to eliminate toxins from the body and reduce body temperature.

Its expectorant properties help to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, making it a useful natural remedy for promoting the removal of mucus and other debris from the respiratory system.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Melissa officinalis is used to treat anxiety by modulating the GABAergic system and reducing symptoms of generalized anxiety disorder. The plant's antispasmodic properties also make it effective in alleviating digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome, by reducing abdominal pain and cramping. Additionally, Melissa officinalis is used to alleviate menopausal symptoms, including hot flashes and night sweats, by regulating the body's temperature and hormone balance. The adaptogenic properties of the plant also help to mitigate stress and promote relaxation, while its sedative effects are used to treat insomnia and sleep disorders, including insomnia and restless leg syndrome. Furthermore, Melissa officinalis is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as sleep disturbances and hypertension, and it has been shown to reduce symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease by decreasing inflammation in the esophagus.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Melissa officinalis is used to treat anxiety by regulating the body's stress response. It is also employed to alleviate digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, due to its carminative properties. Furthermore, Melissa officinalis is utilized to address insomnia and sleep disorders, promoting a restful night's sleep through its sedative effects. Additionally, it is used to reduce fever and mitigate inflammation by modulating the immune response. The plant is also employed to treat a range of other conditions, including stress, respiratory issues, hiccups, and menstrual cramps, which are often related to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Melissa officinalis is used to treat anxiety by modulating the brain's GABA receptors. The herb is also effective in addressing digestive issues, particularly IBS, due to its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. Furthermore, Melissa officinalis has been traditionally used to alleviate sleep disorders, such as insomnia, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, the herb has been employed to alleviate respiratory issues, including coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and mucolytic properties. Its use has also been documented to treat hysteria, hiccups, fever, and various skin conditions, including eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, while also promoting stress relief.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Melissa officinalis is used to treat anxiety, which is often accompanied by insomnia, a condition characterized by difficulty falling or staying asleep. The plant's calming properties also help alleviate digestive issues, including diarrhea, a condition marked by loose, watery stools. Additionally, Melissa officinalis is used to treat hiccups, a sudden, involuntary contraction of the diaphragm, as well as cough, a common symptom of respiratory infections. Its anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating indigestion and fever, which is typically characterized by a high body temperature. Furthermore, Melissa officinalis is used to treat sleep disorders and hysteria, a condition marked by excessive emotionality or irrational behavior.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Melissa officinalis is used to treat anxiety by providing a calming effect due to its high content of rosmarinic acid. This herbal remedy is also effective in managing stress, which can be caused by anxiety, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels. Additionally, Melissa officinalis is used to alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, by regulating bowel movements and reducing inflammation. Furthermore, it is often prescribed for sleep disorders, including insomnia and sleeplessness, as it helps regulate sleep patterns and improve sleep quality. Melissa officinalis is also used to treat menopause symptoms, menstrual cramps, and hives, making it a versatile remedy for various health conditions.
benefits_description: Melissa officinalis reduces inflammation by containing rosmarinic acid, a potent anti-inflammatory compound, and ursolic acid, which inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Acting as antioxidant, Melissa officinalis contains carnosic acid, which scavenges free radicals and protects cells from oxidative damage. Relieving anxiety, Melissa officinalis has been found to possess anxiolytic properties due to its ability to bind to GABA receptors, thereby modulating the activity of the central nervous system. Improving digestion, Melissa officinalis contains apigenin, a flavonoid that enhances gut motility and reduces inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, while treating insomnia, it has been shown to increase the levels of melatonin, a hormone regulating sleep-wake cycles. Soothing respiratory issues, supporting immune function, lowering blood pressure, reducing stress, and preventing cancer, Melissa officinalis contains a complex mixture of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which interact with multiple cellular pathways to exert its therapeutic effects.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its ability to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
The plant's high content of antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid and luteolin, enable it to neutralize free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage, thereby acting as an antioxidant.
The plant's essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may contribute to its anxiolytic properties and ability to relieve anxiety.
The plant's carminative properties, attributed to its essential oils, may help to alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, by reducing gas and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract.
The plant's sedative properties, due to its essential oils, may help to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality by reducing stress and anxiety and regulating sleep-wake cycles.

The plant's essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, have a bronchodilatory effect, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, by relaxing airway muscles and increasing airflow.

The plant's high content of vitamin C and other antioxidants may contribute to its immunomodulatory effects, which help to support the immune system by enhancing its ability to fight off infections and diseases.

The plant's ability to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help to lower blood pressure by reducing vascular resistance and improving cardiac function.
The plant's sedative and anxiolytic properties, due to its essential oils, may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting a sense of calm.

The plant's high content of antioxidants and its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, attributed to its rosmarinic acid and luteolin, may help to prevent cancer by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting cancer cell proliferation.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Melissa officinalis improves blood flow by relaxing vascular smooth muscle, thanks to its vasodilatory properties. Reducing blood pressure is also achieved through the plant's ability to inhibit the activity of the renin-angiotensin system, which regulates blood pressure. Enhancing circulation is facilitated by the plant's antioxidant properties, which protect against oxidative stress and maintain the integrity of blood vessels. Stabilizing heart rate is primarily due to the plant's anti-arrhythmic properties, which counteract the effects of arrhythmogenic substances. The plant's anti-thrombotic properties prevent blood clots by inhibiting platelet aggregation and activation, and by reducing the expression of pro-coagulant genes, thereby boosting oxygen delivery, reducing inflammation levels, supporting capillary health, improving lymphatic function, and protecting against atherosclerosis.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Melissa officinalis relieves bloating due to its carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce gas and alleviate discomfort in the digestive system. The plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby enhancing digestion and allowing for the proper absorption of nutrients. Melissa officinalis soothes irritation in the digestive system with its anti-inflammatory and mucilaginous properties, which coat and protect the mucous membranes. Easing constipation is facilitated by the plant's laxative properties, which stimulate bowel movements and promote regularity. Additionally, Melissa officinalis treats nausea with its antiemetic properties, fights infections with its antimicrobial properties, prevents diarrhea with its astringent properties, and heals ulcers with its mucilaginous properties, while also stimulating appetite with its flavor-enhancing properties.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Melissa officinalis regulates hormones by exerting anxiolytic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and terpenoids, allowing for a balancing effect on the endocrine system. This herb supports thyroid function through its antioxidant activity, neutralizing free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue and impair its function. The plant stabilizes adrenal function by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and free-radical scavenging properties, promoting a healthy adrenal-gland environment. Additionally, Melissa officinalis calms ovarian activity by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and hormonal balancing effects, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with polycystic ovary syndrome. Furthermore, it balances insulin levels by regulating glucose metabolism and reducing oxidative stress, which is achieved through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, ultimately reducing the risk of developing insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Melissa officinalis protects skin from damage by its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress. It reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory property, which inhibits pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. The herb soothes irritation with its antimicrobial properties, preventing the growth of pathogens that cause irritation. Melissa officinalis fights acne by its antibacterial properties, which inhibit the growth of acne-causing bacteria. Calming eczema is achieved through its anti-itching property, which reduces histamine release and itching sensation, and it heals wounds by its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, promoting tissue repair and regeneration.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Melissa officinalis stimulates lymphatic flow by its vasodilatory effect, which increases blood flow and reduces vascular resistance. This plant boosts immune function due to its antimicrobial properties, specifically its ability to inhibit the growth of pathogens such as E. coli and S. aureus. Additionally, Melissa officinalis reduces swollen nodes by its anti-inflammatory action, which decreases the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. The plant enhances detoxification process through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative damage. Furthermore, Melissa officinalis supports lymph node health by its immunomodulatory effect, regulates white blood cell count, improves fluid dynamics, reduces inflammation response, protects against infections, and promotes tissue repair.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Melissa officinalis reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory property, which inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Relieving joint pain is another benefit of Melissa officinalis, attributed to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. This plant improves flexibility due to its antioxidant properties, which protect cells from oxidative stress and promote collagen synthesis. Enhancing muscle strength is achieved through Melissa officinalis's adaptogenic properties, which help regulate muscle contractions and relaxations. By soothing tendonitis, reducing swelling, treating arthritis, curing bursitis, easing sciatica, and healing ligaments, Melissa officinalis demonstrates its comprehensive range of medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic activities.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Melissa officinalis reduces anxiety through its rosmarinic acid content, which exhibits a pronounced anxiolytic effect. The plant calms nerves due to its flavonoid composition, particularly luteolin, which has a sedative action. Melissa officinalis relaxes the brain by modulating the GABAergic system, facilitating the activity of neurotransmitters that promote relaxation. This effect regulates emotions, leading to improved mood, as the plant's essential oil has been shown to increase dopamine and serotonin levels in the brain. By soothing stress and promoting relaxation, melissa officinalis supports sleep, enhances focus, and stabilizes moods, ultimately contributing to a state of well-being.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Melissa officinalis relaxes muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramping that can disrupt the reproductive cycle. This herb regulates hormones by exerting a calming effect on the body, which helps to normalize the endocrine system and promote hormonal balance. By stimulating ovulation, Melissa officinalis increases the likelihood of successful fertilization and conception. The plant's antioxidant properties also boost fertility by protecting reproductive cells from oxidative damage. Additionally, Melissa officinalis reduces stress, which can impede reproductive health, improves libido, enhances endurance, tones the pelvic region, balances the endocrine glands, and supports pregnancy through its anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Melissa officinalis relieves congestion by releasing antimicrobial properties that eliminate bacteria, thereby preventing respiratory infections. This plant reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory compounds, which ease breathing by relaxing airway muscles. Additionally, Melissa officinalis cools respiratory passages with its cooling properties, soothing coughs by reducing irritation and calming inflammation. Its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties also fight infections and decrease mucus production, while its expectorant properties open airways and promote the elimination of phlegm, thereby easing breathing and reducing congestion. Overall, Melissa officinalis is a natural remedy that provides a range of benefits for the respiratory system.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Melissa officinalis prevents kidney stones due to its diuretic properties, which increase urine production and help dissolve kidney stones. The antimicrobial and antifungal properties of Melissa officinalis reduce bladder infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogens that cause these infections. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties treat urinary tract infections by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the urinary tract. Relieving bladder pain is also possible with Melissa officinalis, thanks to its analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties, which reduce pain and spasms in the bladder. Additionally, Melissa officinalis eases urination strains with its antispasmodic properties, cures prostate issues with its antioxidant properties, treats enlarged prostate with its anti-proliferative properties, relieves lower back pain with its anti-inflammatory properties, reduces urinary frequency with its diuretic properties, and prevents urinary incontinence with its strengthening properties of the pelvic floor muscles.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Melissa officinalis are numerous, with its antimicrobial properties being attributed to the presence of essential oils, which inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. The antioxidant properties of this plant help protect cells from oxidative damage, which can be linked to the flavonoids present in its extracts. Additionally, Melissa officinalis exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which are partly due to the saponins found in its roots, contributing to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis. The antispasmodic properties of this plant allow it to ease muscle spasms and cramps, while its anxiolytic properties make it a potential natural remedy for anxiety disorders, likely due to the presence of rosmarinic acid. The antihistamine and antiseptic properties of Melissa officinalis, attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids, help alleviate allergic reactions and prevent the spread of infections, and its antiviral properties are thought to be responsible for its ability to combat viral infections, while its antifungal properties allow it to inhibit the growth of fungi.

properties_list.description: Melissa officinalis has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a variety of microorganisms, including bacteria, fungi, and viruses, through the action of its essential oils and other bioactive compounds.
The plant has antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other polyphenolic compounds, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.

Melissa officinalis has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
The plant has been traditionally used as an antispasmodic, helping to relieve muscle spasms and cramps by relaxing smooth muscle tissue and reducing the excitability of nerve endings.

Melissa officinalis has been reported to possess anxiolytic properties, with studies suggesting that its essential oils and bioactive compounds may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation in individuals with anxiety disorders.

There is limited evidence to suggest that Melissa officinalis has antihistamine properties, although some studies have found that its essential oils may have a slight antihistamine effect in reducing histamine release in vitro.

The plant has been used as an antiseptic, with its essential oils and other bioactive compounds exhibiting antimicrobial activity and helping to prevent the growth of microorganisms on wounds and other surfaces.

Melissa officinalis has been shown to possess antiviral properties, with some studies finding that its essential oils and bioactive compounds may help reduce the replication of certain viruses in vitro.
The plant has been found to exhibit antifungal activity, with its essential oils and bioactive compounds inhibiting the growth of various fungi and helping to prevent fungal infections.

constituents.description: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
A flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties
A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-anxiety properties
Phenolic acids, present in Melissa officinalis, exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Tannins are a type of polyphenolic compound found in Melissa officinalis, characterized by astringency, bitterness, and the ability to bind to proteins and other molecules.
Saponins are a type of triterpene glycoside found in Melissa officinalis, characterized by their foaming, hemolytic, and cytotoxic properties.
Melissa officinalis flavonoids are antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and possess antimicrobial properties.
Quercetin is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.
A mixture of linalool, linalyl acetate, and beta-caryophyllene, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties.
Kaempferol is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Melissa officinalis are a complex mixture of compounds including rosmarinic acid which has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties through its ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Luteolin and apigenin are two flavonoids present in the plant, which have been demonstrated to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, with luteolin also showing potential in inhibiting cancer cell growth and inducing apoptosis. Phenolic acids such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid are also present, contributing to the plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, tannins like epigallocatechin gallate have been identified, which have been reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. These compounds contribute to the plant's therapeutic effects, including its use in treating anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, and its potential in preventing chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Melissa officinalis for medicinal purposes are leaves and flowers. The leaves of Melissa officinalis contain flavonoids, particularly rosmarinic acid, and volatile oils, including luteolin and apigenin, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anxiolytic properties. The flowers of Melissa officinalis are rich in flavonoids, including luteolin and apigenin, as well as volatile oils, such as geraniol and linalool, which have antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anxiolytic properties. The bioactive compounds in both leaves and flowers are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The medicinal properties of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers make them a popular ingredient in herbal teas and supplements.

parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and ursolic acid, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia, and supporting immune function, wound healing, and cardiovascular health.

The flowers of this plant contain flavonoids, particularly luteolin and 7-O-caffeoyl-D-glucoside, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, offering protection against oxidative stress, infections, and inflammation, and promoting relaxation and improved sleep quality.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is a medicinal preparation used to treat anxiety, insomnia, digestive issues, and inflammation, and to soothe stress and irritability due to its high content of rosmarinic acid and melissic acid.
preparations.overview: Melissa officinalis tincture is commonly used to treat anxiety, stress, digestive issues, insomnia, menstrual cramps, sleep disorders, inflammation, hypertension, nausea, and gastroesophageal reflux disease. This herbal preparation is widely used due to its effectiveness in managing various health conditions. A notable effect of Melissa officinalis tincture is its ability to induce relaxation and calmness in individuals. The preparation is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Melissa officinalis plant, which are combined with a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to create a concentrated liquid extract. Making Melissa officinalis tincture requires some knowledge and effort, as it involves careful preparation and monitoring of the solvent-to-plant ratio to ensure the optimal extraction of the desired compounds.
recipe: Gather fresh Melissa officinalis flowers and leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 1 part Melissa officinalis flowers and leaves with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the Melissa officinalis tincture in a cool, dark place and take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparations.description: Suppository made from this plant is used for the local treatment of gastroesophageal reflux disease, peptic ulcers, and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations.overview: Melissa officinalis suppository is an herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including bacterial vaginosis, vaginal dryness, endometriosis, vaginal yeast infection, menstrual cramps, hemorrhoids, urinary tract infections, and cervicitis, with some studies indicating its effectiveness against gastroenteritis. This preparation is relatively uncommonly used due to its limited availability and lack of mainstream recognition. Its effects can vary significantly from person to person, making it challenging to determine its overall efficacy. The suppository is typically made with Melissa officinalis flowers and leaves, which are harvested and then processed into a usable form. The preparation process is complex and requires significant expertise, making it difficult for manufacturers to produce the suppository on a large scale.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and insect bites, as well as to relieve anxiety and insomnia due to its calming effects.
preparations.overview: Melissa officinalis salve is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat a variety of skin conditions, including burns, skin irritation, eczema, insect bites, wounds, wound healing, acne, bruises, and fungal infections, as well as herpes. This preparation is widely used due to its effectiveness in soothing and calming irritated skin. The common effect of Melissa officinalis salve is its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The salve is typically made from the leaves and flowers of the Melissa officinalis plant, which are rich in antioxidants and essential oils. While it may require some skill and patience, the preparation of Melissa officinalis salve is considered moderately difficult, involving a combination of herbal extraction and carrier oil infusion.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves and 1/4 cup of its flowers, and clean them thoroughly.
Steep the leaves and flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the oil mixture, and heat it in a double boiler until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the salve into small containers and let it cool and solidify, then use 1-2 teaspoons as needed, up to 3 times a day.

preparations.description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, digestive issues, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
preparations.overview: Melissa officinalis essential oil is used to treat anxiety, stress, insomnia, digestive issues, skin irritations, inflammation and nausea. It's a common preparation in aromatherapy due to its widespread use. Melissa officinalis essential oil has a common effect of promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The oil is typically made from the leaves and flowers of Melissa officinalis, a plant native to Europe and Asia. The production of Melissa officinalis essential oil is considered challenging due to the low yield of the plant's essential oil and the high cost of steam distillation process required to extract it.
recipe: Gather Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area.
Combine 1 cup of dried Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a clean glass container.
Pour 2 cups of carrier oil (such as jojoba or sweet almond oil) over the dried herb mixture.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily, and then strain it through a cheesecloth.
Store the resulting Melissa officinalis herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle, using a maximum dosage of 1-2 drops per application.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, digestive issues, and as a sedative and anti-inflammatory agent.
preparations.overview: Melissa officinalis tea, also known as lemon balm tea, is widely used to treat anxiety, insomnia, stress, digestive issues, cough, menstrual cramps, nausea, inflammation, skin issues, and hypertension. It's one of the most common herbal teas, often consumed for its soothing effects. Ingestion of Melissa officinalis tea typically results in a calming effect, which is attributed to its rosmarinic acid content. The preparation is made from the leaves and flowers of the Melissa officinalis plant, which are usually steeped in hot water to release the active compounds. Making Melissa officinalis tea is relatively simple, requiring only the plant material and boiling water.
recipe: Harvest fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, using about one tablespoon of the combined herbs for every 8 ounces of water.
Gently rinse the leaves and flowers with cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Steep the Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Drink one to two cups of the Melissa officinalis herbal tea per day, ideally before bedtime, to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, digestion issues, and as an antispasmodic and antiviral agent.
preparations.overview: Melissa officinalis capsule is a widely used herbal preparation, commonly employed to treat anxiety, stress, digestive issues, insomnia, sleep disorders, hypertension, inflammation, depression, nausea, and menstrual cramps. It is a common remedy in traditional medicine due to its widespread availability and long history of use. The preparation typically has a sedative effect, which is likely due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and linalool. Melissa officinalis capsule is made from the leaves and flowers of the Melissa officinalis plant, which are harvested and then processed into a capsule form. The process of making the preparation is moderately difficult, requiring careful handling and processing of the plant material to ensure the desired compounds are preserved.
recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, clean them thoroughly, and dry them in a warm place.
Grind the dried Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the powdered Melissa officinalis, depending on the desired dosage.
Seal the capsules using a capsule filler or by applying gentle heat to the capsule.
Store the Melissa officinalis capsules in a cool, dry place and consume 1-2 capsules per day as needed.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Melissa officinalis for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are often used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to their anxiolytic and sedative properties. Melissa officinalis is also formulated into suppositories to alleviate digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome and inflammatory bowel disease. A salve is also derived from the plant to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and burns, as it has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Essential oil is extracted for aromatherapy, which can reduce stress and anxiety, improve mood, and promote relaxation. Additionally, Melissa officinalis is prepared as a tea to treat anxiety and depression, and it is also available in capsules for digestive health and to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Contact dermatitis occurs upon skin contact with Melissa officinalis extracts.
Interaction with anticoagulant medications increases the risk of bleeding.
Melissa officinalis extracts inhibit cytochrome P450 3A4, increasing plasma concentrations of certain medications.
Melissa officinalis essential oil can cause kidney damage due to the presence of camphor, a nephrotoxin.
Gastrointestinal irritation occurs due to essential oil content in Melissa officinalis.
Melissa officinalis essential oil lowers blood pressure in a dose-dependent manner.
High doses of Melissa officinalis cause liver damage due to liver enzyme elevation and liver histopathology changes.
Large doses of Melissa officinalis essential oil cause headaches in some individuals.
Melissa officinalis may interact with anticoagulant medications, increasing risk of bleeding.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Melissa officinalis are experience allergic reactions, show increased bleeding risk, interact with other medications, worsen kidney function, cause stomach upset, induce hypotension, lead to liver damage, and trigger headaches. Experience allergic reactions may result from an improper dosage, whereas show increased bleeding risk is a concern for individuals taking anticoagulant medications. Interact with other medications by potentially affecting the efficacy of certain medications or exacerbating their side effects. Worsen kidney function can occur in individuals with pre-existing kidney conditions, while cause stomach upset may be a symptom of an allergic reaction or an adverse interaction with other medications. Lead to liver damage in the long term and increase risk of bleeding disorders, especially in individuals with bleeding disorders.

precautions.precaution_description: Use under medical supervision due to interactions with sedatives, diabetes medications, and anticoagulants.
Melissa officinalis is contraindicated in pregnancy due to potential estrogenic effects and possible uterine stimulation.
Individuals with ragweed allergy should assess their sensitivity to Melissa officinalis due to cross-reactivity.
Avoid use with drugs that are metabolized by the liver, as Melissa officinalis may alter liver enzyme activity.
People with diabetes should monitor their blood sugar levels when using Melissa officinalis extracts due to the risk of hypoglycemic effects.
Melissa officinalis essential oil contains thymol, which can cause seizures in infants under 2 years.
Excessive consumption of Melissa officinalis may cause liver damage and interact with certain medications.
Inform healthcare provider about diarrhea, dizziness, and stomach upset

Melissa officinalis may increase the risk of serotonin toxicity when used with SSRIs or MAOIs, and may reduce blood pressure when used with antihypertensive medications, such as beta blockers and diuretics.
Large doses of Melissa officinalis cause drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Melissa officinalis medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional to ensure safe usage, especially for individuals with pre-existing medical conditions. Before initiating treatment, determine pregnancy status to avoid potential harm to the fetus. Assess allergies and evaluate liver function, as Melissa officinalis may exacerbate liver conditions or interact with medications. Monitoring blood sugar levels is also crucial, as it may affect glucose metabolism. Use in moderation and report any side effects, while being aware of the potential for interactions with other medications and overdose symptoms, such as dizziness and nausea, and avoid administering to children under 2 years.
identification: Melissa officinalis is a perennial herbaceous plant with a height of 30-100 cm, featuring oval-shaped leaves 2-5 cm long and 1-2.5 cm wide, with a serrated margin and a short petiole. It has a woody stem with a greyish-brown color and a white, purple, or pinkish-purple inflorescence, consisting of 6-12 tubular flowers 10-15 mm long. The plant is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is widely cultivated in many parts of the world. It thrives in moist, well-drained soils and partial shade to full sun conditions, with a moderate climate. The plant's growth habit is rhizomatous, with a spread of 30-60 cm.
cultivation: Melissa officinalis is a perennial herb that exhibits a spreading growth habit, typically forming a dense mat of stems and roots, and can grow up to 60 cm in height. It prefers well-drained soil with a pH range of 6.0-7.0 and requires full sun to partial shade, with optimal growth temperatures between 15-25°C. To promote healthy growth, it is essential to provide Melissa officinalis with regular watering, avoiding waterlogged soil, and fertilize lightly in the spring and summer months. Mulching around the plants can help retain moisture and suppress weeds, while pruning in the spring can encourage bushy growth and prevent flowering. Regular division of the roots in the spring or fall can also help maintain the plant's vigor and prevent overcrowding.
history: Melissa officinalis is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for over 2,000 years, primarily in ancient Greece and Rome, where it was known as "melissa" or "honey flower" due to its sweet, honey-like fragrance. In medieval Europe, Melissa officinalis was used to treat a range of ailments, including insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues, as well as to alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. The plant's leaves and flowers contain bioactive compounds, including luteolin and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Historically, Melissa officinalis was also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In traditional herbal medicine, Melissa officinalis is still used today to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
